other_observers: None; Kara L. originally found the bird. It stayed for over a week and a lot of birders were able to see and photograph it.
specific_location: It was at Fountain Creek Natural Area south of the headquarters in a small cattail marsh.
description: A dark gray large bill. It has a black head with a white cheek patch and a yellow stripe on top of the head with long yellow plumes coming off the back of the head with a red eye. It had a blue-gray body and neck. It also had yellow legs. The bird overall was fairly small and slim for a heron.Behaviors: It was actively feeding during most of the time I was watching it. Which means it was standing still most of the time waiting for a fish (or other small creature) to swim close to the heron.Call: Didn’t hear itPlumage: Adult
